zotero.abstractNote: Simulation-based inference (SBI) has become a powerful tool for performing Bayesian inference in models where the likelihood is intractable but simulation is possible. However, standard SBI methods often struggle with high-dimensional time series data, as they typically rely on summary statistics or embedding networks that may not capture the temporal dependencies effectively. In this paper, we propose a compositional approach to SBI for time series, which decomposes the inference problem into a sequence of smaller, more manageable tasks. By leveraging the Markov property of the underlying generative process, we construct a compositional posterior estimator that can be trained on short segments of the time series. We demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on a range of challenging time series models, including those with complex temporal dynamics and high-dimensional observations.
